sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Team Standings
sections.text: Osasuna, sitting at 10th in the league with 45 points, hosts Atlético Madrid, who are in an impressive 3rd position with 70 points. This matchup at Estadio El Sadar in Pamplona promises to be a challenging one for the home side. Atlético Madrid’s superior league position reflects their strong season, while Osasuna will be looking to leverage their mid-table stability to cause an upset. The stakes are high as both teams aim to secure crucial points in this La Liga encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 5 matchups, Atlético Madrid have dominated with 4 wins compared to Osasuna’s solitary victory. The most recent clash on January 12, 2025, saw Atlético Madrid edge out a 1-0 win at home, with Clément Lenglet earning the Player of the Match title with a 7.9 Sofascore rating. Osasuna have struggled to keep a clean sheet against Atlético in 13 consecutive head-to-head games. Additionally, Atlético Madrid have been the first to score in 6 of their last 7 encounters, showcasing their ability to take early control.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Osasuna
sections.text: Osasuna will heavily rely on Ante Budimir, their star forward who ranks 3rd in the league for goals scored with 19, surpassing his expected goals of 17.70. Budimir has been in fine form in the last 5 matches, averaging a 7.3 Sofascore rating with 4 goals and 2 assists. Supporting him in attack is Bryan Zaragoza, who has contributed 5 assists despite underperforming his expected goals. In defense, Alejandro Catena stands out with a league rank of 4 for percentage of long balls in total passes at 9.1%, alongside a solid 7.02 average rating.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Atlético Madrid
sections.text: Atlético Madrid boast a lethal attacking duo in Alexander Sørloth and Julián Álvarez, with Sørloth scoring 17 goals and Álvarez adding 15 this season, both overperforming their expected goals significantly. Sørloth and Álvarez have been in top form over the last 5 matches, each averaging a 7.54 Sofascore rating with 6 and 4 goals respectively. Antoine Griezmann, with an average Sofascore rating of 7.2, adds creativity with 6 assists and 8 goals, and has historically performed well against Osasuna with a 7.3 average rating in the last 5 matchups. In defense, Axel Witsel shines with a league-leading 73.9% ground duels won and a 7.02 average rating.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Atlético Madrid enter this match in excellent form, unbeaten in their last 3 games and keeping clean sheets in all of them. Osasuna, on the other hand, have seen both teams score in 5 of their last 6 matches, indicating defensive vulnerabilities but attacking intent. Atlético Madrid have also managed less than 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 matches, suggesting disciplined play. Both teams have consistently recorded less than 10.5 corners in recent games, with Osasuna at 4 of 5 and Atlético at 5 of 5, pointing to potentially limited set-piece action.

sections.subtitle: Osasuna’s Season Statistics
sections.text: Osasuna have scored 43 goals this season, ranking 8th in the league, though they’ve conceded 51, placing them 14th for least goals conceded. They excel in specific areas, ranking 1st in percentage of big chances converted at 44.83% and 2nd in accurate crosses with 195. Their aerial strength is evident with a 4th-ranked 54.82% aerial duels won and 9 headers scored, 5th in the league. However, they struggle with possession at 46.03% (14th) and have committed 479 fouls, ranking 6th for most fouls committed.

sections.subtitle: Atlético Madrid’s Scoring Patterns
sections.text: Atlético Madrid have shown a strong tendency to score late, with 20 of their goals (25.93%) coming in the 76th to 90th-minute period. Their goal distribution is otherwise balanced, with roughly 14.81% of goals scored in each of the other 15-minute intervals across the match. This late-game surge could pose a significant threat to Osasuna, especially as the home side has scored only 23.53% of their goals (5 out of 43) in the same late period. Osasuna’s peak scoring comes between the 16th and 30th minutes, with 10 goals (9.8%).

sections.subtitle: Referee Impact and Disciplinary Expectations
sections.text: Mario Melero Lopez, the referee for this match, averages 5.3 yellow cards per game and 0.19 red cards per game, indicating a tendency for a relatively high number of bookings. Osasuna have accumulated 84 yellow cards this season, ranking 12th for least yellows, while averaging 2.4 per game. Given the referee’s stats and Osasuna’s foul-prone nature (479 fouls, 6th most in the league), coupled with historical head-to-head data showing less than 4.5 cards in 8 of 9 matches between these teams, a moderate number of bookings is likely.
